summary: Full regression on redesigned Lucy landing page + waitlist backend. Backend pytest 9/9 pass. Frontend: hero headline, cycling chat demo, waitlist new+duplicate, footer waitlist, all 4 nav links, header Join Waitlist, sticky Emotion Engine (all 4 panels reached via scroll), Privacy stats, FAQ accordion open/close, mobile 390 layout — ALL PASS. Only issue: 3 dev-console warnings from EmotionEngine SVG paths rendering d='null' on first paint before framer-motion animates.

issues: 3x console error 'Error: <path> attribute d: Expected moveto path command (M or m), null.' — motion.path elements have no initial d prop; first render is d=null before animate kicks in. Cosmetic dev warning, no visible break. Fix: add initial={{ d: emotions[0].eye/mouth }} on the three motion.path elements in EmotionEngine.jsx.

action_items: (low priority) Silence SVG d=null console warnings by giving each <motion.path> in EmotionEngine.jsx an initial d value (e.g. initial={{ d: emotions[0].eye }} for eyes, initial={{ d: emotions[0].mouth }} for mouth).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: WaitlistCreate.source is Optional but has default 'hero'; when payload sends source=null explicitly, `payload.source or 'hero'` handles it — good.
Hero.jsx ChatSequence: setStep depends on step in effect, correct cleanup — good.
WaitlistForm.jsx error toast handles Pydantic 422 array-style detail. Acceptable but could also handle string detail (err.response.data.detail when it's a string) — edge case only.
No file over 700 lines. data-testid coverage is complete for tested flows.
seed_data_creation: TEST_ prefixed waitlist emails inserted in mongo during test run; harmless, isolated by TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 9 tests, all pass. Lenis smooth scroll: page.mouse.wheel() works for advancing emotion panels (confirmed all 4 panels reached). window.scrollTo/scrollIntoView also work fine on this Lenis setup.
rca of the issue: SVG d='null' warning: <motion.path> in EmotionEngine.jsx has animate={{ d: emotion.eye/mouth }} but no `initial` prop, so React renders the element once without a valid d attribute before framer-motion's first animate frame. Browser logs an SVG parse warning but visually recovers immediately.
